key_insight: FormalAnalyticGeo turns analytic-geometry problem creation into a closed-loop neural-symbolic pipeline that generates aligned text, diagrams, formal constraints, and verified answers automatically.
engineering_relevance: The framework provides a scalable way to build high-quality multimodal training data for geometry-focused AI systems without human annotation, which is valuable for benchmark and curriculum generation.
method: Uses CDL as a formal intermediate language between natural-language problems and diagram rendering.
Renders constraint-driven analytic-geometry diagrams with an SDF-based engine for geometric precision.
Chains Generator → Formalizer → Measurer → Quality Verifier with retry feedback to enforce correctness.
evidence_signal: Applied at scale, the system produced AnalyticGeo7K with over 7K verified multimodal problems; the generated set reports a median ground-truth relative error of 0.70% and 82.3% of answers within 5% of the exact symbolic solution.
watch_item: The pipeline’s quality depends on the accuracy of formalization, rendering, and vision-based measurement, so error propagation or edge cases in complex diagrams may still require careful validation.
paper_title: FormalAnalyticGeo: A Neural-Symbolic Based Framework for Multimodal Analytic Geometry Problem Generation
